This tool is for informational and educational purposes only. It is not a substitute for professional medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. Always consult a qualified healthcare provider.

faq.question: What is BMR?
faq.answer: Basal Metabolic Rate – the calories your body burns at complete rest to maintain basic functions.

faq.question: What is TDEE?
faq.answer: Total Daily Energy Expenditure – your BMR multiplied by your activity level. This is your total calorie burn.

faq.question: How many calories to lose weight?
faq.answer: A 500 calorie/day deficit produces ~1 lb/week of weight loss. A 1,000 deficit produces ~2 lbs/week.

faqs.q: How many fahrenheit are in one celsius?

faq.question: What is compound interest?
faq.answer: Interest calculated on both the principal and accumulated interest. Albert Einstein reportedly called it the eighth wonder of the world.

faq.question: How does compounding frequency matter?
faq.answer: More frequent compounding (daily vs annual) slightly increases returns. The difference is small at low rates.

faq.question: What is the Rule of 72?
faq.answer: Divide 72 by your interest rate to estimate years to double your money. At 7%, money doubles in about 10.3 years.

name: Loan Calculator
short_description: Calculate monthly payments for any loan.
long_description: Determine your monthly payment, total interest, and full amortization schedule for personal, auto, student, or business loans. Just enter the loan amount, interest rate, and term.
how_it_works: Enter loan amount, annual interest rate, and loan term in months or years. Click Calculate to see your monthly payment, total interest, and total payment.
example_usage: Example: $20,000 loan at 6.5% for 60 months = $391.32/month, $3,479 total interest.

faq.question: How is a loan payment calculated?
faq.answer: Using the formula: M = P[r(1+r)^n]/[(1+r)^n-1] where P=principal, r=monthly rate, n=number of payments.

faq.question: What is APR?
faq.answer: Annual Percentage Rate – the annual rate charged for borrowing, including fees.

faq.question: Should I choose a shorter or longer loan term?
faq.answer: Shorter terms mean higher monthly payments but less total interest. Longer terms mean lower payments but more interest paid overall.

seo_title: Loan Calculator – Monthly Payment & Amortization | Toolbox.ai
seo_description: Calculate your monthly loan payment, total interest, and amortization. Free loan calculator for any loan type.

long_description: Find out how extra payments reduce your loan payoff date and total interest. Compare minimum payment vs accelerated repayment strategies.
how_it_works: Enter your current balance, interest rate, minimum payment, and any extra monthly payment. Results show payoff date and total interest saved.
example_usage: Example: $15,000 credit card at 22% APR, paying $400/month vs minimum = pay off 3 years sooner, save $8,000.

faq.question: How does paying extra principal help?
faq.answer: Extra principal payments reduce the balance faster, which means less interest accrues over time.

faq.question: Should I pay off debt or invest?
faq.answer: If debt interest rate exceeds expected investment returns, paying off debt is usually the better choice.

faq.question: What is the avalanche vs snowball method?
faq.answer: Avalanche: pay highest-rate debt first. Snowball: pay smallest balance first for psychological wins.

faq.answer: Principal, Interest, Taxes, and Insurance – the four components of a mortgage payment.

faq.question: Should I put 20% down?
faq.answer: 20% down avoids PMI (private mortgage insurance), but many loans allow less. PMI typically adds 0.5-1.5% annually.

faq.answer: Fixed rates stay constant for the loan life. ARMs change after an initial period, introducing risk but often lower initial rates.

faq.answer: Organic mulch breaks down in 1-2 years. Refresh annually or when depth drops below 2 inches.

faq.answer: A typical 2-cubic-foot bag covers about 8 sq ft at 3 inch depth. You need about 14 bags per cubic yard.

how_it_works: Enter the first day of your last menstrual period (LMP) or your conception date. Results show estimated due date and current week.
example_usage: Example: LMP January 10, 2026 = Due date approximately October 17, 2026.

faq.question: How is the due date calculated?
faq.answer: Naegele's Rule: add 280 days (40 weeks) to the first day of the last menstrual period.

faq.question: How accurate is the due date?
faq.answer: Only about 5% of babies arrive on their exact due date. Most deliver within 2 weeks of the EDD.

faq.question: What is a full-term pregnancy?
faq.answer: Between 39 and 40 weeks and 6 days. Babies born between 37-38 weeks are early term.
